source.notes: Two-tank hydraulic system with bistable pipe-flow hysteresis (relaxation oscillator). Tank 1 (5-ft diameter, 24-inch height) fills at a configurable inflow rate. The outlet port is a pipe whose geometry is modelled explicitly: part-full (open-channel, air-entrained) vs pressurized (primed, full elevation head). The pipe primes when submergence above its crown exceeds S_crit × D, and breaks when the crown is exposed. For 2.5-inch diameter pipe and 5 L/s inflow this produces a relaxation oscillation: Tank 1 level rises slowly on part-full flow, jumps to primed flow that drains it faster than it fills, then collapses back when the crown is exposed — period ≈ 8 min (steady-state with Tank 2 full).

elements.name: Inflow Rate (L/s)
elements.description: Constant inflow rate to Tank 1 in litres per second. Editable.

elements.name: Pipe Diameter (in)
elements.description: Inner diameter of the connecting pipe in inches. At 2.5 in and 5 L/s inflow, the part-full capacity is below Q_in but the pressurized capacity exceeds it, satisfying the relaxation-oscillator condition. Editable.

elements.name: A_tank
elements.description: Cross-sectional area of each cylindrical tank. Diameter = 5 ft, A = π × 2.5² = 19.635 ft².

elements.name: h_outlet
elements.description: Height of the pipe invert (bottom of pipe opening) above Tank 1's base: 12 in = 1 ft.

elements.name: delta_z
elements.description: Elevation drop from Tank 1's base to Tank 2's base: 2 ft. When the pipe is primed this full head drives flow.

elements.name: f_darcy
elements.description: Darcy-Weisbach friction factor for smooth PVC pipe: f ≈ 0.025.

elements.name: L_hose
elements.description: Length of the connecting pipe: 10 ft.

elements.name: g_gravity
elements.description: Standard gravitational acceleration: 32.174 ft/s².

elements.name: pi
elements.description: Mathematical constant π.

elements.name: S_crit
elements.description: Critical submergence ratio (Gordon/Knauss type). The pipe primes when the depth of water above the pipe crown exceeds S_crit × D. Typical range 1.0–1.5.

elements.name: C_d_partial
elements.description: Discharge coefficient for the pipe inlet in the part-full (unprimed, air-entrained) regime. Treated as a submerged orifice at the pipe entrance: C_d ≈ 0.61.

elements.name: K_switch
elements.description: Rate at which the pipe_prime state variable is driven toward 0 or 1 once a threshold is crossed (s⁻¹). At dt=5s a value of 0.5 transitions pipe_prime across [0,1] in one step — effectively instantaneous.

elements.name: break_sub
elements.description: Submergence above crown (ft) at which prime breaks: crown just exposed (0 ft). In practice vortex break can occur at small positive submergence; raise to e.g. 0.1×D to widen the hysteresis zone.

elements.name: Q_in
elements.description: Inflow rate in ft³/s. Converted from L/s: 1 ft³ = 28.3168 L.

elements.name: d_hose_ft
elements.description: Pipe inner diameter in feet.

elements.name: A_hose
elements.description: Pipe bore cross-sectional area: π × (D/2)² = π × D² / 4 [ft²].

elements.name: LD_ratio
elements.description: Pipe length-to-diameter ratio: L / D. Drives the Darcy friction head-loss term.

elements.name: K_hose
elements.description: Total pipe loss coefficient (pressurized regime): K_entry(0.5) + f×L/D + K_exit(1.0). At 2.5": 1.5 + 0.025×48 = 2.7.

elements.name: C_hose
elements.description: Pressurized-regime discharge coefficient: C = A_pipe × √(2g / K_hose) [ft^2.5/s]. Q_primed = C_hose × √H_eff.

elements.name: h_port_crown
elements.description: Height of the pipe crown (top of pipe opening) above Tank 1's base: h_outlet + D. The priming threshold and break threshold are both referenced to this level.

elements.name: h_above_crown
elements.description: Current submergence of the pipe crown: h1 − h_port_crown [ft]. Negative when crown is exposed (definitely unprimed). Positive drives toward priming.

elements.name: prime_threshold
elements.description: Submergence above the crown required to suppress air entrainment and prime the pipe: S_crit × D [ft].

elements.name: prime_rate
elements.description: Rate of change of pipe_prime state [s⁻¹]: +K_switch when priming condition met (h_above_crown > prime_threshold), −K_switch when breaking condition met (crown exposed), 0 in hysteresis zone. The zero in the hysteresis zone is what makes pipe_prime bistable.

elements.name: h_eff_unprimed
elements.description: Driving head for the unprimed (air-entrained) regime [ft]. This is only the static head above the pipe invert — the pipe cannot 'see' the 2-ft elevation drop while air-entrained.

elements.name: H_eff_primed
elements.description: Driving head for the pressurized (primed) regime [ft]. Full elevation difference: (h1 − h2) + delta_z. Once primed the pipe 'sees' the entire 2-ft drop plus the level difference.

elements.name: Q_unprimed
elements.description: Flow in the part-full, air-entrained regime [ft³/s]. Orifice formula with the shallow head only: Q = C_d_partial × A_pipe × √(2g × h_eff_unprimed). Much less than Q_in once the level stalls in the hysteresis zone.

elements.name: Q_primed
elements.description: Flow in the pressurized (primed) regime [ft³/s]. Darcy-Weisbach full-pipe formula: Q = C_hose × √H_eff_primed. Substantially exceeds Q_in, draining Tank 1 faster than it fills.

elements.name: Q_hose
elements.description: Actual flow through the pipe [ft³/s]. Weighted blend of the two regimes by pipe_prime ∈ [0, 1]: Q = pipe_prime × Q_primed + (1 − pipe_prime) × Q_unprimed. Because pipe_prime transitions in one timestep the blend is effectively a hard switch.

elements.name: pipe_prime
elements.description: Pipe flow-regime state variable ∈ [0, 1]. 0 = part-full (air-entrained); 1 = pressurized (primed). Driven toward 1 when h_above_crown > prime_threshold, toward 0 when crown is exposed (h_above_crown < break_sub), and frozen at its current value inside the hysteresis zone — this frozen-state is what makes it bistable and produces the relaxation oscillation.

elements.name: h1
elements.description: Water depth in Tank 1 above its base [ft]. Net rate = (Q_in − Q_hose) / A_tank. Oscillates: rises on part-full flow (Q_hose < Q_in), falls once primed (Q_hose > Q_in), bounded [0, 2 ft].

elements.name: h2
elements.description: Water depth in Tank 2 above its base [ft]. Receives Q_hose; overflows to ground when full. Base sits 2 ft lower than Tank 1.
